title: SoFi Stock Analysis: Strong Business, Valuation Concerns
text: SoFi Technologies stock has plummeted nearly 40% year-to-date, despite posting what many consider its strongest quarter ever. The company achieved record revenue of $1.1 billion (41% year-over-year growth) and added a record 1.1 million members, bringing its total to 14.7 million. The fintech platform demonstrated impressive metrics: adjusted EBITDA climbed 62% to $340 million with a 31% margin, GAAP net income more than doubled to $167 million, student loan originations more than doubled, and home loans increased 2.4x year-over-year. CEO Anthony Noto noted the company achieved the 18th consecutive quarter of the Rule of 40 with a score of 72. Despite strong operational performance, valuation remains a primary concern. The stock trades at approximately 27 times management's full-year 2026 earnings guidance of $0.60 per share, a substantial premium for a consumer lending-focused business historically vulnerable to economic downturns. Additional headwinds include the technology/banking-as-a-service segment declined 27% year-over-year, a March short-seller report from Muddy Waters Research raised accounting concerns, and personal loan charge-off rates ticked upward to 3.03%. The underlying business appears healthy, but current valuation may not justify entry at current price levels.
